description: Tables accessed with complex index math should be declared as extern u8 TABLE[] and byte-offset computed manually
trigger: Target uses sll/subu/addu chain for array offset, not simple indexed load
solution: Declare table as u8[], compute offset as (field1 * stride1) + (field2 * stride2)

description: When target loads arg from stack with lbu, the parameter should be u8 not s32
trigger: Target has lbu from stack offset matching an argument position

description: IDO allocates registers in declaration order. Reordering locals swaps register assignments
trigger: Generated asm has correct instructions but swapped register numbers
solution: Reorder local variable declarations to match target register numbering

description: Naming float locals explicitly steers FPR allocation to match target load order
trigger: Float expression produces correct result but FP registers differ from target
solution: Name float locals matching their FPR (temp_f2, temp_f12, temp_f14) in target load order

description: Pointer-based do/while produces tighter loop matching for string/byte iterations
trigger: Target uses pointer increment + branch-likely for byte loop
solution: Use explicit pointer variable with do { ptr++; } while (*ptr != 0) instead of index

description: Cascading if/return chains match IDO's branch layout for switch-like functions
trigger: Target has sequential bne/beqz with jr $ra in delay slots
solution: Use if (arg == N) { *out1 = X; *out2 = Y; return; } pattern

description: Assignment in if-condition can produce beql/bnel that matches branch-likely targets
trigger: Target uses branch-likely (beql/bnel) with store in delay slot

description: Callers that don't explicitly pass $a0 rely on it surviving into the callee
trigger: Callers call func() with no args but the callee clearly uses $a0
solution: Declare the parameter in the callee signature; callers keep $a0 from context
